Title: Biosynthesis and function of polyacetylenes and allied natural products

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Microsomal oleoyl-phosphatidylcholine Δ12 desaturase; FAD2 family
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Linoleic acid / precursor to acetylenic fatty acids
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor for unusual fatty acid and defense-related polyacetylene biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies linoleate precursor and serves as progenitor family for diverged acetylenases
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA tagging/cloning and comparative functional studies cited in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAD2 was cloned from Arabidopsis and provided the conceptual foundation for identifying diverged acetylenases. 2. Its product linoleate is the direct substrate for Crep1-like acetylenases. 3. Endogenous FAD2 activity may also contribute to downstream dehydrocrepenynate formation in heterologous systems.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competition between FAD2/FAD3 and diverged FAD2 enzymes affects unusual fatty acid accumulation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important precursor-supply gene for engineering acetylenic fatty acid pathways
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated here as background/common rather than a core experimental target in original studies.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: jasmonate precursor supply and oxidative sink
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Competes with acetylenases for 18:2 substrate, potentially limiting acetylenic fatty acid biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: real-time RT-PCR correlation discussed in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAD3 competes with acetylenases for linoleate substrate. 2. Higher FAD3 demand for 18:2 may limit acetylenase output in flowers. 3. It influences the balance between oxylipin/jasmonate and acetylenic lipid production.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Diverts flux away from unusual fatty acid synthesis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to rebalance substrate allocation when engineering acetylenic lipids
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a common competitive pathway gene, not a focal acetylenase.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Monounsaturated acyl-ACP products; precursors to petroselenic/tariric pathways
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Native AAD1 variants establish front-end unsaturation patterns that can feed unusual acetylenic pathways
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative biochemical characterization summarized in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAD1 normally performs Δ9 desaturation on acyl-ACP in plastids. 2. Variants with altered regioselectivity underlie front-end desaturation routes relevant to tariric/petroselenic-type pathways. 3. Sequence changes of only a few residues can shift regioselectivity and substrate preference.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Scaffold for engineering novel ACP-directed acetylenases/desaturases
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a likely progenitor framework for front-end acetylenase evolution, but not itself a core acetylenase in this review.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transfers acetylenic acyl groups from PC to DAG/TAG, affecting accumulation and detoxification of unusual fatty acids
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cloned functional characterization discussed in review; enzymatic substrate preference assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtPDAT transfers acyl groups from PC to DAG and can use crepenynoyl-PC at about half the rate of 18:2. 2. It prefers short-chain, oxygenated, and unsaturated acyl groups and thus may help channel unusual fatty acids into TAG. 3. It likely influences the extent of acetylenic fatty acid storage versus membrane retention.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alters partitioning between membrane lipids and storage oils
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important auxiliary gene for improving accumulation of engineered acetylenic oils
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common_Gene because it controls lipid flux rather than catalyzing acetylenation or transcriptional regulation.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Desaturase-like membrane protein related to Ole1p; lacks cytochrome b5 fusion
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Jamaicamide acetylenic fatty acyl starter unit
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to perform terminal desaturation/acetylenation on a C6 fatty acid starter used for jamaicamide biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cluster analysis; homology; pathway reconstruction; biochemical support from JamA substrate preference
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JamB is a desaturase-like enzyme embedded in the jamaicamide PK-NRPS cluster. 2. It is proposed to generate the terminally unsaturated/acetylenic starter unit from 5-hexenoic or 5-hexynoic acid-related substrates. 3. It represents a likely desaturase/acetylenase linked directly to a microbial PK pathway.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: gene cluster sequencing, no accession given
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable lead for discovering PK-linked acetylenase systems and engineering terminal alkynes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function is proposed rather than directly biochemically proven in the review, so classified as Common_Gene rather than Pathway_Gene.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: seed-specific promoter in Arabidopsis; yeast heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Crepenynate pathway / acetylenic fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Δ12 acetylenation branch of linoleate-derived polyacetylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FAD2-like microsomal membrane-bound desaturase; 4 hydrophobic segments; 3 histidine boxes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in developing seeds; much lower in flowers
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Linoleic acid; also oleic acid for desaturase side activity
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Crepenynic acid; also octadecadienoic acid isomers including 18:2(9Z,12E)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts linoleate to crepenynate by oxidative dehydrogenation introducing an acetylenic bond at C12-C13; also shows Δ12 desaturase/trans-desaturase activity on oleate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms key acetylenic pathway intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control/committed branch-forming step for crepenynate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables production of crepenynic acid and initiates acetylenic fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Arabidopsis; metabolite profiling; mutational/sequence comparison; kinetic isotope effect analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Crep1 from C. alpina was the first cloned plant Δ12 acetylenase, confirming the desaturase hypothesis for alkyne formation. 2. Heterologous expression produced crepenynate from linoleate and trans/cis 18:2 products from oleate, demonstrating bifunctional desaturase/acetylenase activity. 3. Expression is seed-enriched, correlating with high crepenynic acid accumulation in developing seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed development; heterologous expression with exogenous linoleic acid in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible competition with FAD3 for linoleate substrate; unusual fatty acid accumulation may be limited by degradation/targeting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering acetylenic seed oils and polyacetylene pathways in crop plants; likely requires optimization of substrate supply and lipid flux
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review states Crep1 also forms trans-18:2 products and has broadened binding pocket features linked to acetylenase chemistry; KIE indicates asynchronous H abstraction initiated at C12.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Acetylenic fatty acid biosynthesis in Apiaceae
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Δ12 acetylenation leading to crepenynate and dehydrocrepenynate
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FAD2-like membrane desaturase with transmembrane domains and histidine boxes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: developing soybean seed embryos in heterologous assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes acetylenation of endogenous linoleate to crepenynate in heterologous seed embryos; pathway output includes dehydrocrepenynate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key branch step, not explicitly rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers accumulation of acetylenic fatty acids from endogenous linoleate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: elicitor-responsive expression analysis; heterologous expression in soybean embryos; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ELI12 is Pep25-inducible in parsley and encodes a FAD2-like protein. 2. Although inactive in yeast, expression in soybean embryos led to crepenynate and dehydrocrepenynate accumulation. 3. Supports a role in pathogen-responsive acetylenic fatty acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: EST comparisons mentioned, no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering pathogen-inducible acetylenic lipid formation in crops
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source of apparent Δ14 desaturase activity in soybean embryos was not resolved; endogenous FAD2 activity may contribute.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Front-end desaturase with N-terminal cytochrome b5 domain and histidine boxes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: storage acetylenic TAG reserve in protonemata
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Δ6-unsaturated fatty acid precursor generated by front-end desaturation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces Δ6 unsaturation and acetylenic functionality in bryophyte fatty acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms acetylenic fatty acid product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Responsible for moss-specific Δ6 acetylenic fatty acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; sequence analysis; heterologous expression/functional assignment as summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPAcet6 from C. purpureus encodes a bifunctional Δ6 desaturase/acetylenase. 2. It contains an N-terminal cytochrome b5 domain distinct from plant Δ12 acetylenases. 3. Unlike Crep1-like enzymes, it can produce its own Δ6-unsaturated substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Expands engineering options to front-end acetylenation chemistry distinct from FAD2-like enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Low similarity to other known Δ6 desaturases suggests independent evolution of moss acetylenase function.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Front-end desaturase with N-terminal cytochrome b5 domain
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Δ6-unsaturated fatty acid precursor for acetylenic lipids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor supply for acetylenic fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a Δ6 double bond to generate substrate for moss acetylenic fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the unsaturated precursor required for CPAcet6-mediated acetylenation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning and functional characterization summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPDes6 is the companion Δ6 desaturase cloned from C. purpureus. 2. It provides the Δ6-unsaturated precursor needed for downstream acetylenation. 3. Together with CPAcet6 it defines bryophyte front-end acetylenic lipid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be stacked with acetylenase genes to build front-end acetylenic fatty acid pathways
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review presents it as one of the paired core genes functionally characterizing the moss acetylenic pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cyclopropyl/cyclopropenyl fatty acid biosynthesis allied to unusual fatty acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cyclopropane formation in sterculic acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cyclopropyl fatty acid synthase fused to N-terminal flavin-binding domain
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Dihydrosterculic acid / sterculic acid pathway intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: phosphatidylcholine in seed microsomes; seed lipids
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: sn-1 oleoyl groups of phosphatidylcholine; S-[14CH3]methionine as methyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methylene from methionine-derived donor to oleoyl-PC forming cyclopropane fatty acid 5N
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Creates cyclopropane intermediate required for sterculic acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EST screening; microsomal localization/activity assay; substrate feeding with radiolabeled methionine and oleate; heterologous expression in tobacco cells
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SfCPA-FAS was isolated from developing S. foetida seeds and localized to microsomes. 2. It converts oleoyl-PC, mainly at the sn-1 position, to the cyclopropane intermediate dihydrosterculate. 3. Expression in tobacco did not produce cyclopropenyl fatty acids, indicating downstream enzymes remain unidentified.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: EST library screening, no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering cyclopropane fatty acids, but downstream desaturase and acyl transfer steps are still missing
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not an acetylenase but treated in the review as a core cloned gene in allied unusual fatty acid biosynthesis relevant to acetylenic pathways.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for conversion of enynoic acid intermediates to acetylated pheromone products
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent essential activator of final pheromone formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: labeling/pathway studies with hormonal activation comparisons summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PBAN enables conversion of enynoic acid to acetylated pheromone in T. pityocampa. 2. Pheromone production is hormonally regulated in both T. pityocampa and T. processionea. 3. Species differences in acetylenase presence/absence combine with PBAN control to determine pheromone output.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: final pheromone biosynthetic steps downstream of enynoic acid
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates pheromone biosynthetic conversion to acetate product
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as the only explicit regulatory factor discussed as controlling acetylenic metabolite production; not plant-related.
